stimulus: The composer Teresa Carreño made significant contributions to the development of nineteenth-century Venezuelan classical music, but her work is rarely performed in concert halls in Europe today except among specialists in Latin American composition. One reason for Carreño’s relative obscurity in Europe is the towering reputation of certain contemporaries. In particular, the composers Heitor Villa-Lobos and Manuel de Falla have so dominated European programming of Latin and Iberian repertoire that Carreño and other Latin American composers of the period are frequently overlooked, despite the technical mastery and originality of their writing.
stem: Which choice best states the main idea of the text?

answerOptions.content: During Carreño’s lifetime, her compositions were programmed in European concert halls far more often than the works of Villa-Lobos and de Falla.
Carreño composed pieces on some of the same themes that Villa-Lobos and de Falla treated but employed sharply different harmonic techniques.
Carreño’s work is less widely performed in Europe than it deserves to be because Villa-Lobos and de Falla draw so much of audiences’ attention.
Few of Carreño’s scores survive today because she produced only a small number of complete compositions.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. The text states that Carreño made significant contributions but is rarely performed in Europe and explains that Villa-Lobos and de Falla so dominate programming that Carreño is overlooked. That is the main idea.

Choice A is incorrect because the text doesn’t compare performance frequencies during Carreño’s lifetime; it actually emphasizes her current obscurity. Choice B is incorrect because the text does not discuss the themes or harmonic techniques of Carreño or her contemporaries. Choice D is incorrect because the text never claims that Carreño produced only a few works or that her scores are scarce.
